title: Gendo Ikaris way of thinking and dealing with things
display_title: Gendo Ikaris way of thinking and dealing with things
wikitext:
[]: Gendo Ikari's way of thinking and dealing with things             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Thu Jul 13, 2006 11:13 pm I wonder if Gendo has ever loved someone. He used Yui to get to the posistion of power, used his son to accomplish his own conspiracy and even Rei was his toy. I wonder how can such an untrusting person ever live on earth. Doesn't he feel insecure? Hardly ever any expressions on his face. What was his way of dealing with things?

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Thu Jul 13, 2006 11:19 pm Really do think he loved Yui, but that got twisted. It still boggles the mind how he can try to move heaven and earth to be with her again and not see that he his wife would have demanded he take better care of their son.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Fri Jul 14, 2006 12:12 am I always thought Gendo loved Yui because she was the only one who loved him, and when she left he wanted to be with her too be loved again. At the same time he didn't think anyone but him could love him so he never bothered to try to reciprocate the freindship of those who extended the offer of freindship and love in Ritsuko's case. He used and abused those around him to get what he wanted. I don't think he had mankind's best interest in mind but merely his own.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Jul 14, 2006 12:14 am I just dont get how you can clam to love a women and no do what she would so blaringly want you to do. Look after your child

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Fri Jul 14, 2006 12:30 am I get the feeling he didn't want Shinji to begin with. As soon as she died he ditched the kid onto his brother....and he was off screwing around in Antarctica while Shinji was little. And when Shinji was in the lab the day of Yui's death Gendo wasn't even paying attention to him causing Naoko to be rather PO'd.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Fri Jul 14, 2006 2:24 am That he was off in Antartica doesn't really mean much, so he had a 'buisness' trip. He left his son and wife for something, excuse me, rather big. That doesn't really mean that much. On topic, though, I think he did love Yui. He wanted her back in his arms, and was never truly capable of accepting the world as it was without Yui-hence why he wanted to 'move heaven and earth'. He was a man desperate for the woman he loved, and who loved him. When the two met, Yui said Gendo was 'cute' and that he has a 'good soul'. That reminds me quite a bit of Kaworu and his meeting Shinji, putting Shinji and Gendo in the same place. It also helps put Kaworu and Yui on the same line of the two people who truly loved Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Fri Jul 14, 2006 2:29 am Gendo has a brother?

[]:             Quote Post by Light of the Dawn » Fri Jul 14, 2006 2:45 am I guess he loved Yui for real. It seems like he loved Shinji too while Yui was around, but her disappearance kinda broke him apart and he kept Shinji far away from him. In my opinion he did love Yui and Shinji, just had a hard way of showing that.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Fri Jul 14, 2006 2:56 am TheBlueTree wrote: Gendo has a brother?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jul 14, 2006 3:20 am Legend of the Past wrote: Yes, Shinji's famous 'Sensei'.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Fri Jul 14, 2006 10:20 am SEELE 08 wrote: ....and he was off screwing around in Antarctica while Shinji was little.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Fri Jul 14, 2006 11:35 am The feeling I get is that Gendo has vowed to reunite with Yui. And apart from every other case in the world, for him, it is not entirely impossible. That much should be obvious. And even as he regains his cool, he's still devoted enough to his plan for himself and Yui to disregard everything else. So he seems like a sort of psychopath to me, and acts like one most of the time. Wether this is forced or not, I do not know, but it seems to be working out fine for him. Another thing I have no idea why it happens is why Rei rejects Gendo at the very end. Is it Lilith deeming him unworthy to join in?

[]:             Quote Post by defiate » Fri Jul 14, 2006 11:44 am kaysow]The feeling I get is that Gendo has vowed to reunite with Yui. And apart from every other case in the world, for him, it is not entirely impossible. That much should be obvious. And even as he regains his cool, he's still devoted enough to his plan for himself and Yui to disregard everything else. [/quote] Yup that is correct. All of Gendo's motives are explained in EoE when he gets "complimented". In his words, everything he did up to his complimentation was for him to meet Yui again. The feeling I get is that Gendo has vowed to reunite with Yui. And apart from every other case in the world, for him, it is not entirely impossible. That much should be obvious. And even as he regains his cool, he's still devoted enough to his plan for himself and Yui to disregard everything else. [quote="Kaysow wrote: So he seems like a sort of psychopath to me, and acts like one most of the time. Wether this is forced or not, I do not know, but it seems to be working out fine for him.

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Fri Jul 14, 2006 11:54 am defiate wrote: "Ikari is calling" says Rei as she rejects Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by defiate » Fri Jul 14, 2006 11:56 am FauÃŸtin wrote: Which Ikari: Shinji, or Yui? This is the only question for me around this topic. As I recall, Ayanami was made with the use of Yui's DNA - though my memory is quite dizzy, as usual. So both Shinji and Yui are her relatives. I dare not say a word, since I have absolutely no idea - this is why I'm asking.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Jul 14, 2006 12:54 pm FauÃŸtin wrote: defiate wrote: "Ikari is calling" says Rei as she rejects Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Fri Jul 14, 2006 1:28 pm She calls him 'Ikari-Kun', hinting Ikari his her equal. Besides, Yui should be reffered to as Sama. :P

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jul 14, 2006 2:19 pm Legend of the Past wrote: She calls him 'Ikari-Kun', hinting Ikari his her equal.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Fri Jul 14, 2006 2:43 pm If Yui wouldn't be in Unit 01, Shinji wouldn't pilot and the decision wouldn't be placed upon his shoulders. If SEELE's design would be succesful, mankind would just stay a big vat of jello in a black, hollow rock. Though, it's intresting what was Gendo's vision of 3I. Maybe he wanted to grant mankind the immortality the Fruit of Life offered along with the knowledge they already had? That would 'turn mankind to God', and would allow him to reunite with Yui, assuming the power of Tree of Life would allow her to restore her body.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jul 14, 2006 2:55 pm thewayneiac wrote: SEELE 08 wrote: ....and he was off screwing around in Antarctica while Shinji was little.
